Items.Title: DevOpsDays 2023 布拉格 | DevOpsDays 2023 Prague
Items.Description:
现在是星期二一大早，我又坐上了地铁，前往布拉格一个我以前从未去过的地方。 火车上的人们很安静，都在思考自己的人生。 我看到只有几个人不看手机。 我们是否已经对一些闪亮的设备和所谓的“技术”上瘾了？ 那可能是另一个话题。 现在，我要去布拉格 DevOpsDays2023 的第二天了。

酒店欢迎我，外面有许多吸烟者和一个奇怪的外观。 我来对地方了，因为我看到了熟悉的面孔，听到有人在谈论詹金斯。

我乘电梯来到会议区，前往登记处。 收到欢迎礼包后，一个可爱的女孩向我打招呼。 我回头打招呼。 我告诉自己出事了。 看，可爱的女孩子从来不会那样跟我打招呼。

然后我想起我在一家友好的公司，因为 DevOps 不仅涉及技术，还涉及对他人友善的心态。

我遇到了几个和我一起工作的好人，然后去了会议室。 我期待更多的与会者。 房间已满 80%。 现在可能还为时过早。

我正好赶上第一个发言者。

我在试图说服我的一些利益相关者我们正在运送不太安全的集装箱时发现了他们的免费扫描仪。 我渴望了解更多。

他展示了两组的安全发现，倾向于毕业项目更安全一点的意图（在大多数地区）。 我们应该研究他们是怎么做到的，并应用到我们自己身上。

我一直在努力解决的另一个令人兴奋的问题是，我们应该调查在扫描过程中发现的关键/高 CVE，因为它们在特定情况下无法被利用。 通常，这是补救活动的一部分，但我知道有些公司无论如何都希望容器干净。 这种斗争有时会在团队内部引起很多摩擦。 他还展示了一些用例来支持这一点。

我不希望人工智能现在能做到这一点，但这将是未来。

考虑到标题，第二个演讲会很无聊。 我错了。

保罗带领我们经历了一段成长之旅，他向我和大多数听众提出挑战，让他们思考多样性、社区和真正的归属感。

尽管他的谈话是关于在不同国家的一些努力，但我学到了很多关于工作和建立社区的知识。 这是您（职业）生活工具带上的重要工具。

我是一个视觉人。 当他分享一张停车场草地的照片时，我立马想到：一切皆有可能。 然后他用自己的话来说了同样的话。

我们需要寻找可能性，特别是在不寻常的地方。 生活之美不在于已知。 没有人会在乎满是混凝土的海滩，但大多数人会微笑并高兴地看到一朵花冲破恐惧，为世界带来美好。 这也适用于个人和工作关系。 正确的？

当他在布拉格时，我想花更多的时间和保罗在一起，但我没有做到。 他是一个很有洞察力的人，值得学习。 所以下次你参加他附近的活动时，打个招呼并吸收一些智慧。

休息;

在任何活动中，我最喜欢的部分之一就是“免费”咖啡。 好吧，你明白我的意思了，这是“免费”啤酒，但现在是 10:30，而且我不喜欢在早上喝酒。

茶歇时间是通过参观赞助商的展位来支持社区会议的绝佳方式。 因为他们，这样的事件才会存在。

休息时间的第二个最佳用途是结识新朋友。 我宁愿避免结识新朋友。 大多数时候，当我不得不和一个完全陌生的人交谈时，我会感到紧张。 我知道我很奇怪。 通过与我已经认识的人交谈，我留在了自己的舒适区。 如果您喜欢结识新朋友，可以利用茶歇时间扩大您的视野。

喝了一杯恶心的咖啡后，我回到了房间。 速溶咖啡和我喜欢的不一样，但我需要咖啡因。 我回到房间去见下一位出色的演讲者。

好吧，没关系

进行适当的测试是一个骗局。 我们总是以一种违背质量团队内部价值和经验的方式来做这件事。 另一方面，同一个团队所需的测试级别可能在业务上更具合理性。 当然，这是我唯一的意见。

在现代 DevOps 世界中，测试的优先级非常低。

每个人都知道我们需要发布更快的高质量软件。 令人惊讶的是，需要解决负责这部分的团队。 他们对改进、工具和流程的建议通常放在 /dev/null 中。

我从这次谈话中学到了很多东西。 如果我必须指出一个：自动化是关键，但您需要与您的质量专业人员交谈才能实现这一点。 那你为什么不现在开始这个对话呢？

你还能确保你已经完成了整个 9 码的测试吗？

将 Sec 部分放入 DevSecOps 的安全组件非常庞大。 通常人们可能会因为我们的目标是同时关注许多事情而对他们的知识感到不安全。

关键是要确定要关注的领域以及您在知识方面的差距并制定学习计划。

心理健康至关重要。 照顾好自己并寻求帮助。 中场休息

两杯意式浓缩咖啡后，我厌倦了写我在 DevOpsDays 上的精彩经历。 我的能量水平越来越低，我现在想去遛狗。 当我这样做时，请反思今天的学习：

  • 开源是安全的； 您可以效仿一些很好的例子。 接下来你打算采取什么步骤？
  • 你可以在不寻常的地方找到美丽和鼓舞人心的想法。 这对你来说代表着什么？
  • 我们可以在不进行测试的情况下交付高质量的软件吗？ 谁能告诉？
  • 持续学习是战胜冒名顶替综合症的绝佳方式。 接下来你要学什么？

你为什么不也走一小段路呢？ 开放空间

我回来了。 我希望你在户外有一个令人耳目一新的休息时间。 我不太喜欢商业会议，因为你总是有更多的问题和话题，而且没有讨论的场所。

我呆了三届，但我贡献了两届。

通常，拉斯维加斯规则适用，所以我不会分享我们讨论的内容，但我可以自由分享我学到的东西：

  1. 一个话题可能会爆发出更多的话题，使整个谈话没有重点和浪费。 房间的主要话题是沟通中的性别差距，但我们最终对整个多元化和包容性主题进行了热烈讨论，但我们一无所获。

  2. 我参观的第二个开放空间叫做“商界人士在哪里”。 会议室需要帮助来确定他们为什么需要商务人员以及为什么他们有兴趣找到他们。 我们尚未就本届会议的目标达成一致。 我们分享了一些人们去的地方的活动名称，仅此而已。

  3. 第三个讨论是关于冒名顶替综合症以及如何克服它。 会议南下，很多人无法发表自己的意见，浪费了好意。

在开放空间中放置一些微小的结构并将能量集中在正确的方向上可以产生对在场的每个人都有益的美丽结果。 我见过这个工作！

荣誉

我乘地铁回到我所在的布拉格地区，仍然观察着周围的人。 我好奇。 我充满活力和新知识回到了家。 我知道组织这样的活动有多难。 我是根据经验说的。 我已经组织了其中的 30 多个。

感谢DevOpsDays 2023的全体工作人员，他们在空闲时间这样做，除了为其他人提供一个会面和讨论共同话题的场所之外，没有任何好处。

你们应该为自己感到骄傲！

我还在我的软件交付博客上发表了这篇文章。



原文:

It’s early Tuesday morning, and I am again in the metro, going to a location in Prague I have never been to before. People are quiet on the train, thinking about their lives. I saw just a few people not looking at their phones. Have we become humanity addicted to some shiny devices and so-called “technologies”? That could be another topic. Now, I am going to the second day of DevOpsDays2023 Prague.

The hotel welcomes me with many tobacco smokers outside and a strange facade. I am in the right place because I saw familiar faces and heard someone talking about Jenkins.

I took the elevator to the conference area and headed to the registration. A lovely girl said hello to me after I got my welcome pack. I said hello back. I told myself something was wrong. See, sweet girls never say hello to me like that.

Then I remembered I am in a friendly company because DevOps is not only about technologies but also about the mindset of being nice to others.

I met a few great people I worked with and then went to the conference room. I expected more attendees. The room was 80% full. It may be too early.

I was just on time for the first speaker.

Ben Hirschberg – Unpacking Open Source Security in Public Repos & Registries.

Ben is representing a company called ARMO which deals with container security. I discovered their free scanner while trying to convince some of my stakeholders that we are shipping not-so-well-secured containers. I was eager to learn more.

Armo’s team researched and compared the vulnerabilities they discovered in two large groups: All open-source projects in Github as one group and a group of so-called “graduated projects” from CNCF as the second. He showed the security findings in both groups, leaning towards the intention that the graduated projects are a bit more secure (in most areas). We should investigate how they did it and apply it on our own.

The other exciting point I have struggled with is that we should investigate critical /high CVEs found during the scan because they could not be exploited in particular cases. Usually, this is part of the remediation activity, but I know of some companies that want their containers clean, no matter what. This struggle sometimes causes a lot of friction within the teams. He also showed some use cases to support that.

The CVE scanners are good conversation starters, but the human element is needed to do the analysis and prepare the remediation plan. I don’t expect the AI to do this now, but this will be the future.

Paul Bruce – Re-growing a DevOps Community (in Boston)

Considering the title, the second talk would be boring. I was wrong.

Paul took us through a growth journey, and he challenged me and most of the audience to think about diversity, community, and true belonging.

Even though his talk was about some efforts in a different country, I learned a lot about working and building a community. This is a vital tool on your tool belt of (professional) life.

I am a visual person. When he shared a picture of grass in the parking lot, I immediately thought: everything is possible. Then he said the same with his own words.

We need to look for possibilities, specifically in unusual places. The beauty of life doesn’t lie in the known. Nobody cares about a beach full of concrete, but most people will smile and be happy seeing a flower pushing through the horror and bringing something beautiful to the world. This also applies to personal and work relations. Right?

I wanted to spend more time with Paul while he was in Prague, but I didn’t make it. He is a very insightful person to learn from. So next time you end up in an event near him, say hi and soak some wisdom.

Break;

One of my favorite parts of any event is the “free” coffee. Ok, you got me, it’s the “free” beer, but it was 10:30, and I don’t like drinking in the morning.

The coffee breaks are an excellent way to support community conferences by visiting sponsors’ booths. Because of them, such events exist.

The second best use of the breaks is to meet people. I prefer to avoid meeting new people. I get nervous most of the time when I have to talk to an entirely stranger person. I know I am weird. I stayed within my comfort zone by talking to people I already knew. If you like meeting people, use the coffee break to enlarge your horizon.

I went back to the room after gulping a disgusting cup of coffee. Instant coffee is different from my thing, but I needed the caffeine. I went back to the room to meet the next fantastic speaker.

Sowmya Sridharamurthy – TestOps? well, never mind

Doing proper testing is a hoax. We always do it in a way that goes against the value and the experience within the quality team. On the other hand, the level of testing desired by the same team could be more business justifiable. This, of course, is my sole opinion.

Sowmya focuses on an exciting intersection between Operations and Testing. Testing has a very low priority in the modern DevOps world.

Everyone knows that we need to ship faster quality software. Surprisingly the team responsible for this part needs to be addressed. Their proposals for improvements, tools, and processes often go to /dev/null.

I learned a lot from this talk. If I have to pinpoint one: Automation is the key, but you need to talk to your quality professionals to achieve that. So why don’t you start this conversation now?

Can you also ensure you have covered the whole 9 yards of testing?

Michael Man – Overcoming the DevSecOps Imposter Syndrome

The Security component that puts the Sec part into the DevSecOps is vast. Often one might feel insecure in their knowledge just because we aim to focus on many things simultaneously.

Michal covered how he solved such a problem himself. The key is to identify what area to focus on and the gaps you have in your knowledge and prepare a learning plan.

Mental health is essential. Take care of yourself and reach out for help. The Cuber Security community will help you.

Intermission

Two cups of espresso later, I get tired of writing about my great DevOpsDays experience. My energy level is getting low, and I want to go and walk the dog now. While I am doing this, please reflect on the learnings from today:

  • Opensource is secure; There are good examples you could follow. What steps are you going to take next?
  • You could find beauty and inspirational ideas mostly in unusual places. What does that mean to you?
  • Can we deliver quality software without testing? Who can tell?
  • Continuous learning is an excellent way to beat the Impostor syndrome. What are you going to learn next?

Why don’t you take a short walk as well? See you in 15 minutes.

Open Spaces

I am back. I hope you had a refreshing break in the open. I’m not too fond of commercial conferences because you always have many more questions and topics, and there is no venue to discuss them.

DevOpsDays Prague fixes that by running an Open Space where you can propose your topic, get the people interested in discussing it, and a room or a space where you do that.

I stayed for three sessions, but I contributed to two.

Usually, the Vegas rule applies, so I am not going to share what we have discussed, but I am free to share what I have learned:

  1. One topic could explode into more subjects, making the whole conversation unfocused and wasteful. The main topic of the room was Gender gaps in communications, but we ended up in a heated discussion about the entire Diversity and Inclusion subject, and we achieved nothing.

  2. The second open space I visited was called “Where are the Business people.” The room needed help to identify why they needed business people and why they were interested in finding them. We have yet to reach an agreement on the goal of the session. We shared some event names where people go, and that’s all.

  3. The third discussion was about the Impostor Syndrome and how to overcome it. The meeting went south, and many people could not share their opinions, wasting good intentions.

Putting some tiny structures in the Open Spaces and focusing the energy in the right direction could produce beautiful results beneficial for everyone present. I’ve seen this working!

Kudos

I took the metro back to my area of Prague, still observing people around me. I am curious. I went back home full of energy and new knowledge. I know how hard it is to organize an event like this. I am speaking from experience. I have organized more than 30 of those.

Kudos to the whole crew of DevOpsDays 2023, who did this in their free time without any benefits except for the joy of providing the others with a place to meet and discuss common topics.

You should be proud of yourselves!

I also published this article on my software delivery blog.

Items.Title: 5 软件工程原理：构建现代技术的基础 | 5 Principles of Software Engineering: Building the Foundation of Modern Technology
Items.Description:
介绍：

在不断发展的技术世界中，软件工程在塑造和革新我们的生活方面发挥着关键作用。 从智能手机到自动驾驶汽车，软件工程是这些创新的核心，能够创建高效、可靠且用户友好的软件系统。 但软件工程的原理究竟是什么？ 在本文中，我们将探索指导软件开发的基本原则，确保其质量、可扩展性和可维护性。

  1. 抽象原则：
    软件工程的核心是抽象原则。 抽象是通过将复杂系统分解为可管理的模块化组件来简化复杂系统的过程。 这使软件工程师可以专注于特定功能并设计不同软件模块之间的清晰接口。 通过采用抽象，开发人员可以创建高度可维护和可重用的代码，从而促进高效协作并增强软件可靠性。

  2. 模块化原则：
    模块化是软件工程的另一个重要原则。 它涉及将软件系统划分为独立的模块，每个模块负责特定的功能。 这种模块化方法增强了整个系统的灵活性，因为各个模块可以独立开发、测试和更新。 模块化还提高了代码的可重用性，使构建和维护复杂的软件系统变得更加容易。 此外，模块化设计允许更好的错误隔离和故障排除，减少错误的影响并实现高效调试。

  3. 封装原理：
    封装是将数据和相关功能捆绑到称为对象的单个实体中的做法。 这一原则促进了信息隐藏，因为对象封装了它们的内部状态，并且只公开了一个定义良好的接口来与其他对象进行交互。 封装增强了软件安全性，因为它限制了对敏感数据的访问并防止未经授权的修改。 此外，它提高了代码的可维护性和可扩展性，因为对对象内部实现的修改不会影响系统的其他部分。

  4. 内聚原则：
    内聚性是指软件模块内相关性和统一性的程度。 高内聚是可取的，因为它表明模块内的功能密切相关且重点突出。 这一原则确保每个模块都有明确的目的并执行单一的、定义明确的任务。 高内聚简化了代码理解、测试和维护。 它还减少了意外副作用的可能性并促进了代码重用。

  5. 松耦合原则：
    松耦合与紧耦合相反，强调软件模块的独立性和灵活性。 松散耦合确保模块以最小的依赖关系互连，减少一个模块中的更改对其他模块的影响。 这一原则使软件系统的修改、更新和可扩展性变得更容易。 它还允许团队同时在不同模块上工作而无需持续协调，从而促进并行开发。 松散耦合促进了代码的可修改性和系统弹性。

结论：
软件工程原理为开发健壮、可扩展和可维护的软件系统提供了坚实的基础。 抽象、模块化、封装、内聚和松散耦合是指导软件工程师构建高质量软件的基本原则。 通过坚持这些原则，开发人员可以创建

灵活、可重复使用并适应不断变化的技术环境。 随着技术的不断发展，软件工程原则对于确保未来创新的成功和可靠性仍然至关重要。

在接下来的文章中，我们将详细探讨这些原则中的每一个，提供真实世界的应用程序和随附的示例代码以加深理解。 通过探索实际示例，我们旨在展示如何将这些原则有效地应用到软件工程中，使您能够更有效地掌握它们的意义和实现。

Introduction:

In the ever-evolving world of technology, software engineering plays a pivotal role in shaping and revolutionizing our lives. From smartphones to self-driving cars, software engineering is at the core of these innovations, enabling the creation of efficient, reliable, and user-friendly software systems. But what exactly is the principle of software engineering? In this article, we will explore the fundamental principles that guide the development of software, ensuring its quality, scalability, and maintainability.

  1. The Principle of Abstraction:
    At the heart of software engineering lies the principle of abstraction. Abstraction is the process of simplifying complex systems by breaking them down into manageable, modular components. This allows software engineers to focus on specific functionalities and design clear interfaces between different software modules. By employing abstraction, developers can create highly maintainable and reusable code, which promotes efficient collaboration and enhances software reliability.

  2. The Principle of Modularity:
    Modularity is another crucial principle of software engineering. It involves dividing a software system into self-contained modules, each responsible for a specific functionality. This modular approach enhances the overall system's flexibility, as individual modules can be developed, tested, and updated independently. Modularity also promotes code reusability, making it easier to build and maintain complex software systems. Additionally, modular design allows for better error isolation and troubleshooting, reducing the impact of bugs and enabling efficient debugging.

  3. The Principle of Encapsulation:
    Encapsulation is the practice of bundling data and related functions into a single entity called an object. This principle fosters information hiding, as objects encapsulate their internal state and expose only a well-defined interface for interacting with other objects. Encapsulation enhances software security, as it limits access to sensitive data and prevents unauthorized modifications. Moreover, it promotes code maintainability and extensibility, as modifications to the internal implementation of an object do not affect other parts of the system.

  4. The Principle of Cohesion:
    Cohesion refers to the degree of relatedness and unity within a software module. High cohesion is desirable, as it indicates that the functionality within a module is closely related and focused. This principle ensures that each module has a clear purpose and performs a single, well-defined task. High cohesion simplifies code understanding, testing, and maintenance. It also reduces the chances of unintended side effects and promotes code reuse.

  5. The Principle of Loose Coupling:
    Loose coupling is the opposite of tight coupling, emphasizing the independence and flexibility of software modules. Loose coupling ensures that modules are interconnected with minimal dependencies, reducing the impact of changes in one module on others. This principle enables easier modification, updates, and scalability of software systems. It also facilitates parallel development by allowing teams to work on different modules simultaneously without constant coordination. Loose coupling promotes code modifiability and system resilience.

Conclusion:
The principle of software engineering provides a solid foundation for developing robust, scalable, and maintainable software systems. Abstraction, modularity, encapsulation, cohesion, and loose coupling are essential principles that guide software engineers in building high-quality software. By adhering to these principles, developers can create software that is

flexible, reusable, and adaptable to the ever-changing technological landscape. As technology continues to evolve, software engineering principles will remain vital in ensuring the success and reliability of future innovations.

In the following articles, we will delve into each of these principles in detail, providing real-world applications and accompanying sample code to enhance understanding. By exploring practical examples, we aim to demonstrate how these principles can be effectively applied in software engineering, enabling you to grasp their significance and implementation more effectively.

Items.Title: AWS CDK for Go 的特点（与 TypeScript 的区别） | Features of AWS CDK for Go (differences from TypeScript)
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我想写一下 Golang 的 AWS CDK 与 TypeScript 相比的特点。

假设

此处提供的 Golang 代码使用 v1.18.7。

我们将在本文中讨论的 AWS CDK for Go 的特性主要是与 TypeScript 版本的不同之处。

可以在此处的文档中找到基本功能。

特性（与 TypeScript 的区别）

初始化时的目录结构

在cdk init app --language go之后创建的目录结构，以 Go 为例，如下所示。

名为cdk-go目录（存储库）。

 ❯ tree . ├── README.md ├── cdk-go.go ├── cdk-go_test.go ├── cdk.json └── go.mod

对于TypeScript， bin 、 lib 、 test目录是分开的，在init的同时根据package.json安装modules，同时生成node_modules 。

初始化时的文件内容

在Go中，只生成cdk-go.go和cdk-go_test.go Go文件，如上图。

在 TypeScript 中，堆栈生成代码写在bin生成的文件中，堆栈定义代码写在lib生成的文件中，但在 Go 中，两者一起写在同一个文件 ( cdk-go.go ) 中。

这是cdk-go.go的初始代码。

 package main import ( "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2" // "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2/awssqs" "github.com/aws/constructs-go/constructs/v10" "github.com/aws/jsii-runtime-go" ) type CdkGoStackProps struct { awscdk.StackProps } func NewCdkGoStack(scope constructs.Construct, id string, props *CdkGoStackProps) awscdk.Stack { var sprops awscdk.StackProps if props != nil { sprops = props.StackProps } stack := awscdk.NewStack(scope, &id, &sprops) // The code that defines your stack goes here // example resource // queue := awssqs.NewQueue(stack, jsii.String("CdkGoQueue"), &awssqs.QueueProps{ // VisibilityTimeout: awscdk.Duration_Seconds(jsii.Number(300)), // }) return stack } func main() { defer jsii.Close() app := awscdk.NewApp(nil) NewCdkGoStack(app, "CdkGoStack", &CdkGoStackProps{ awscdk.StackProps{ Env: env(), }, }) app.Synth(nil) } // env determines the AWS environment (account+region) in which our stack is to // be deployed. For more information see: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/latest/guide/environments.html func env() *awscdk.Environment { // If unspecified, this stack will be "environment-agnostic". // Account/Region-dependent features and context lookups will not work, but a // single synthesized template can be deployed anywhere. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- return nil // Uncomment if you know exactly what account and region you want to deploy // the stack to. This is the recommendation for production stacks. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- // return &awscdk.Environment{ // Account: jsii.String("123456789012"), // Region: jsii.String("us-east-1"), // } // Uncomment to specialize this stack for the AWS Account and Region that are // implied by the current CLI configuration. This is recommended for dev // stacks. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- // return &awscdk.Environment{ // Account: jsii.String(os.Getenv("CDK_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT")), // Region: jsii.String(os.Getenv("CDK_DEFAULT_REGION")), // } }

结构CdkGoStackProps具有函数NewCdkGoStack 、 main和env 。

函数env在 TypeScript 版本中没有找到，但是 Go 版本特有（从头开始编写），并返回一个awscdk.Environment结构体，其中包含Account和Region 。

对了，初始化时的测试代码在这里。

 package main // import ( // "testing" // "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2" // "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2/assertions" // "github.com/aws/jsii-runtime-go" // ) // example tests. To run these tests, uncomment this file along with the // example resource in cdk-go_test.go // func TestCdkGoStack(t *testing.T) { // // GIVEN // app := awscdk.NewApp(nil) // // WHEN // stack := NewCdkGoStack(app, "MyStack", nil) // // THEN // template := assertions.Template_FromStack(stack) // template.HasResourceProperties(jsii.String("AWS::SQS::Queue"), map[string]interface{}{ // "VisibilityTimeout": 300, // }) // }

看上面的代码，有些地方，尤其是指定参数的时候， jsii.Number 、 jsii.String等函数把参数指定的值包围起来了。

 queue := awssqs.NewQueue(stack, jsii.String("CdkGoQueue"), &awssqs.QueueProps{ VisibilityTimeout: awscdk.Duration_Seconds(jsii.Number(300)), })

实际上，这是 Go 版本的 CDK 中指定参数时的一个规则。

因为 Go 语言没有允许 null 的类型，唯一可以包含nil类型就是指针类型。 因此，要实现可选参数的“无属性”，我们可以传递一个可以为nil指针类型（参见上面的文档）。

这是主要的使用区域。

  • 这是CDK的Go版本的一大特色，也是比较麻烦的，但是一旦习惯了，就没什么了（？）。 我认为这不是问题，一旦你习惯了。 在TypeScript版本中，我认为是CamelCase，但是在Go版本中，基本上CDK中提供的字段和方法名都是PascalCase（以大写字母开头） （见上面文档）。

    这是 Go 的语言规范，Public（包外）调用只能使用 Pascal 大小写调用。

    在 Go 中编码时，如果范围是 Private（正确地说，一个包），它将是 CamelCase。

    这是一个示例：当您尝试通过复制将用 TypeScript 编写的示例带入 Go 时要小心。

     cfnAppRunner.SetHealthCheckConfiguration(&awsapprunner.CfnService_HealthCheckConfigurationProperty{ Path: jsii.String("/"), Protocol: jsii.String("HTTP"), })

    不能做逃生舱口。

    是的，我在逃生舱门上遇到了麻烦。 这是Go版本特有的一个非常大的问题。

    首先，CDK 有一个名为 L2 Construct 的便捷功能，它允许您通过简单地指定一些必需的值来根据最佳实践构建资源。

    还有 L1 Construct，它允许您通过以与 CloudFormation 一致的方式指定资源来构建资源。

    因此，有一种方法可以将使用 L2 Construct 创建的资源转换为 L1 Construct 类型，然后覆盖只能使用 L1 指定的参数，这就是“逃生舱口”。

    但是，根据workshop 的说法，Go 的 CDK 目前不支持逃生舱口。

    不幸的是，CDK for Go 尚不支持此功能，因此我们必须通过 L1 构造来创建资源。 请参阅此 GitHub 问题以获取更多信息并关注 Go 中对 CDK 逃生舱口支持的进展。

    那你怎么办，如果你想指定一个L2 Construct中不能指定的参数，它必须是L1 Construct？

    与此同时，有一种方法可以做一个逃生舱口。 您可以使用jsii.Get方法来完成。

     var cfnAppRunner awsapprunner.CfnService jsii.Get(apprunnerServiceL2.Node(), "defaultChild", &cfnAppRunner) cfnAppRunner.SetAutoScalingConfigurationArn(autoScalingConfigurationArn)

    但是，这个jsii.Get已被弃用（请参阅此页面），所以我觉得我别无选择，只能使用它。

    快照测试

    您可以在 TypeScript (javascript) 中使用jest来完成，但在 Go 中，您可以按如下方式进行。

    从堆栈生成模板并将 JSON 化版本传递给cupaloy.SnapshotT以执行快照。

     stack := NewAppRunnerStack(app, "AppRunnerStack", appRunnerStackProps) template := assertions.Template_FromStack(stack, nil) templateJson := template.ToJSON() t.Run("Snapshot Test", func(t *testing.T) { cupaloy.SnapshotT(t, templateJson) })

    额外的

    下面与其说和TypeScript区别不大，倒不如说是Go特有的一种开发方式。

    工作区模式

    使用 CDK 进行开发时，您可能有多个代码组。

    首先是CDK代码组，然后是Lambda和ECS使用的代码组。

    在这种情况下，可以在一个存储库中准备这些目录并通过在根目录中共享 go.mod 来构建它们，但是您需要准备一个结合每个目录所需内容的 go.mod（多模块） .

    这允许使用 go.mod 为每个子目录进行多模块开发和构建。

    点击以下命令将使您进入工作区模式。

     go work init moduleA moduleB

    然后将创建以下 go.work 文件以启用多模块开发。

     go 1.18 use ( ./moduleA ./moduleB )

    示例实现

    如果您想查看，我使用 AWS CDK for Go 制作了一个实际示例。 最后

我总结了我使用 Go 的 CDK 的经验，因为我发现它与 TypeScript 版本有很大不同。

老实说，它和 CDK 的 TypeScript 版本相比还处于起步阶段，但我还是很高兴能用 Go 来写！



原文:

AWS CDK is often written in TypeScript, but can be written in a variety of programming languages.

In this article, I would like to write about the features of AWS CDK by Golang compared to TypeScript.

Assumptions

The Golang codes presented here use v1.18.7.

The AWS CDK version is v2.52.0.

The features of AWS CDK for Go that we will discuss in this article are mainly the differences from the TypeScript version.

The basic features can be found in the document here.

Features (differences from TypeScript)

Directory structure at init

The directory structure created after cdk init app --language go, in the case of Go, is as follows.

The directory (repository) named cdk-go.

❯ tree
.
├── README.md
├── cdk-go.go
├── cdk-go_test.go
├── cdk.json
└── go.mod

In the case of TypeScript, the bin, lib and test directories are separated, and modules are installed based on package.json at the same time as init, and node_modules are also generated.

Go is much simpler and has no hierarchical structure, which is probably due to the flat directory structure often used when developing in Go.

File contents at init

In Go, only cdk-go.go and cdk-go_test.go Go files are generated, as shown above.

In TypeScript, the stack generation code is written in the file generated in bin and the stack definition code in the file generated in lib, but in Go, both are written together in the same single file (cdk-go.go).

This is the initial code of cdk-go.go.

package main import ( "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2" // "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2/awssqs" "github.com/aws/constructs-go/constructs/v10" "github.com/aws/jsii-runtime-go"
) type CdkGoStackProps struct { awscdk.StackProps
} func NewCdkGoStack(scope constructs.Construct, id string, props *CdkGoStackProps) awscdk.Stack { var sprops awscdk.StackProps if props != nil { sprops = props.StackProps } stack := awscdk.NewStack(scope, &id, &sprops) // The code that defines your stack goes here // example resource // queue := awssqs.NewQueue(stack, jsii.String("CdkGoQueue"), &awssqs.QueueProps{ // VisibilityTimeout: awscdk.Duration_Seconds(jsii.Number(300)), // }) return stack
} func main() { defer jsii.Close() app := awscdk.NewApp(nil) NewCdkGoStack(app, "CdkGoStack", &CdkGoStackProps{ awscdk.StackProps{ Env: env(), }, }) app.Synth(nil)
} // env determines the AWS environment (account+region) in which our stack is to
// be deployed. For more information see: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/latest/guide/environments.html
func env() *awscdk.Environment { // If unspecified, this stack will be "environment-agnostic". // Account/Region-dependent features and context lookups will not work, but a // single synthesized template can be deployed anywhere. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- return nil // Uncomment if you know exactly what account and region you want to deploy // the stack to. This is the recommendation for production stacks. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- // return &awscdk.Environment{ // Account: jsii.String("123456789012"), // Region: jsii.String("us-east-1"), // } // Uncomment to specialize this stack for the AWS Account and Region that are // implied by the current CLI configuration. This is recommended for dev // stacks. //--------------------------------------------------------------------------- // return &awscdk.Environment{ // Account: jsii.String(os.Getenv("CDK_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT")), // Region: jsii.String(os.Getenv("CDK_DEFAULT_REGION")), // }
}

The struct CdkGoStackProps has functions NewCdkGoStack, main and env.

NewXxxStack is the stack definition (in lib in TypeScript) and main is the stack generation (in bin in TypeScript).

The defer jsii.Close() in the main() function is to make the CDK application clean up automatically (see official reference above).

The function env is not found in the TypeScript version, but is unique to the Go version (written from the beginning), and returns an awscdk.Environment structure with Account and Region.

By the way, the test codes at initialization are here.

package main // import (
// "testing" // "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2"
// "github.com/aws/aws-cdk-go/awscdk/v2/assertions"
// "github.com/aws/jsii-runtime-go"
// ) // example tests. To run these tests, uncomment this file along with the
// example resource in cdk-go_test.go
// func TestCdkGoStack(t *testing.T) {
// // GIVEN
// app := awscdk.NewApp(nil) // // WHEN
// stack := NewCdkGoStack(app, "MyStack", nil) // // THEN
// template := assertions.Template_FromStack(stack) // template.HasResourceProperties(jsii.String("AWS::SQS::Queue"), map[string]interface{}{
// "VisibilityTimeout": 300,
// })
// }

Pointer conversion with jsii

Looking at the above code, in some places, especially when specifying parameters, functions such as jsii.Number, jsii.String surround the value specified by the parameter.

 queue := awssqs.NewQueue(stack, jsii.String("CdkGoQueue"), &awssqs.QueueProps{ VisibilityTimeout: awscdk.Duration_Seconds(jsii.Number(300)), })

The jsii.Number and jsii.String are functions that only return a pointer to the variable you give them.

In fact, this is a rule when specifying parameters in the Go version of CDK.

Because the Go language does not have a null-allowed type, and the only type that can contain nil is the pointer type. (The word "only" may be misleading, but we are not going to go into Go here, but translate the above document.)

So, to realize "no property" for an optional parameter, we can pass a pointer type that can be nil (see document above).

This is the main area of use.

  • jsii.String
  • jsii.Number
  • jsii.Bool
  • jsii.Time

This is one of the major and troublesome features of the Go version of the CDK, but once you get used to it, it is nothing(?). I think it's not a problem once you get used to it. (It will make the outlook worse, but...)

Field and method names in CDK module are Pascal case

In the TypeScript version, I think it is CamelCase, but in the Go version, basically the field and method names provided in CDK are PascalCase (beginning with a capital letter) (see the above document).

This is Go's language specification that Public (out-of-package) calls can only be invoked with Pascal case.

When coding in Go, if the scope is Private (properly, a package), it will be CamelCase.

Here's an example: be careful when you try to bring a sample written in TypeScript to Go by copying it.

cfnAppRunner.SetHealthCheckConfiguration(&awsapprunner.CfnService_HealthCheckConfigurationProperty{ Path: jsii.String("/"), Protocol: jsii.String("HTTP"),
})

Cannot do escape hatch.

Yes, I am having trouble with the escape hatch. This is a very big problem unique to the Go version.

First, CDK has a convenient feature called L2 Construct that allows you to build resources according to best practices by simply specifying a few required values.

There is also L1 Construct, which allows you to build resources by specifying them in a manner consistent with CloudFormation.

While L2 Construct is convenient and easy to create resources, it also abstracts parameters, so there are some parameters that are not specified in L2.

Therefore, there is a way to cast a resource created with L2 Construct to an L1 Construct type and later override the parameters that can only be specified with L1, which is the "escape hatch".

However, the CDK for Go does not currently support escape hatch according to the workshop.

CDK has a concept called the escape hatch , which allows you to modify an L2 construct's underlying CFN Resource to access features that are supported by CloudFormation but not yet supported by CDK. Unfortunately, CDK for Go does not yet support this functionality, so we have to create the resource through the L1 construct. See this GitHub issue for more information and to follow progress on support for CDK escape hatches in Go.

So what do you do, if you want to specify a parameter that can't be specified in L2 Construct, it has to be L1 Construct?

In the meantime, there is a way to do an escape hatch. You can do it by using the method jsii.Get.

var cfnAppRunner awsapprunner.CfnService
jsii.Get(apprunnerServiceL2.Node(), "defaultChild", &cfnAppRunner) cfnAppRunner.SetAutoScalingConfigurationArn(autoScalingConfigurationArn)

However, this jsii.Get is deprecated (see this page), so I feel like I have no choice but to use it.

Snapshot test

The "snapshot test" is a test that you will almost certainly see as a CDK test.

You can do it with jest in TypeScript (javascript), but in Go, you can do it as follows.

Generate a template from the stack and pass the JSONized version to cupaloy.SnapshotT to execute the snapshot.

 stack := NewAppRunnerStack(app, "AppRunnerStack", appRunnerStackProps) template := assertions.Template_FromStack(stack, nil) templateJson := template.ToJSON() t.Run("Snapshot Test", func(t *testing.T) { cupaloy.SnapshotT(t, templateJson) })

Extra

The following is not so much a difference from TypeScript, but more like a development method unique to Go.

Workspaces mode

When developing with CDK, you probably have multiple code groups.

First, there is the CDK code group, and then there is the code group used for Lambda and ECS.

In such cases, it is possible to prepare those directories in one repository and build them by sharing go.mod in the root directory, but you will want to prepare a go.mod that combines what is needed in each one (multi-module).

(By the way, in TypeScript, you can use aws-lambda-nodejs to get a nice bundle of Lambda and CDK definitions in a single package.json file, respectively.)

Go introduced a feature called "Workspaces mode" in version 1.18 that allows multi-module (easier than before).

This allows for multi-module development and build with a go.mod for each subdirectory.

Hitting the following command will put you in Workspaces mode.

go work init moduleA moduleB

The following go.work file will then be created to enable multi-module development.

go 1.18

use (
    ./moduleA
    ./moduleB
)

Example Implementation

There is an actual example that I made with AWS CDK for Go, if you would like to see it. (In the example, we are making App Runner.)

Finally

I have summarized my experience with Go's CDK because I found it to be quite different from the TypeScript version.

To be honest, it is still in its infancy compared to the TypeScript version of CDK, but I am still glad to be able to write in Go!

拉维·阿赫达

发表于 6 月 15 日

什么是 DS_Store？

  • 它被称为桌面服务商店，它包含有关文件夹缩略图、设置等的元信息。

为什么？

  • 每当您从 Mac 上的 Finder 导航到文件或文件夹时，都会创建这些文件。

如何摆脱它？

  • 可能您已经知道解决方案，.gitignore

原文:

Ravi Agheda

Posted on Jun 15

 

Mac user generally face this issue where .DS_Store file gets generated automatically and pops up in the git changes ( unless you've added it in ignore 😅 )

What is DS_Store?

  • It known as Desktop Services Store and it holds meta information about your folder’s thumbnails, settings, etc.

Why?

  • These files are created any time you navigate to a file or folder from the Finder on a Mac.

How to get rid of it?

  • Probably you already know the solution, .gitignore
